mnemonics: SIGALRM is your ALARM clock; Pause() is hitting the SNOOZE button until it goes off.
keyPoints: The alarm() function schedules a SIGALRM signal to be sent to the process after a specified number of seconds.
The pause() function suspends the calling process until any signal is caught and its handler returns.
Using sleep() can be implemented with a combination of alarm() and pause(), but this is error-prone if other parts of the code also use alarm().
commonMistakes: Assuming alarm() will delay program execution; it only schedules a signal, it does not pause the process.
Forgetting that alarm() will overwrite any previously set alarm without delivering the pending SIGALRM.
Creating race conditions by calling alarm() again before the previously set signal is handled.
